problem_summary: AI-generated repair tutorial: fixing the flat front tire of a vintage blue-and-white Decathlon Rockrider mountain bike.
safety_pre_check: Stabilize the bicycle before starting the repair.
Fully deflate any remaining air before removing the tire.
Keep fingers away from sharp debris when inspecting the inside of the tire.
Test the front brake before riding after reassembly.

parts_summary.name: Replacement inner tube
parts_summary.specification: 26-inch inner tube matching the tire width, valve type to match the wheel, likely Schrader

parts_summary.name: Valve cap
parts_summary.specification: Reuse the existing cap if available

tools_summary.name: Bicycle pump
tools_summary.specification: Compatible with the valve type on the replacement tube

scene_lock.subject: A vintage Decathlon Rockrider mountain bike with a blue front section, white rear section, black saddle, black bottle cage, front suspension fork, and V-brakes. The damaged part is the front wheel only.
scene_lock.environment: Outdoor repair setting matching the source photo, with a white corrugated metal wall in the background, rough gray ground, and soft natural daylight.
scene_lock.hands_style: Adult hands wearing fitted black nitrile mechanic gloves.
scene_lock.style: Photorealistic instructional video style with realistic bicycle geometry, natural daylight, high mechanical detail, and clean step-by-step visual clarity.
scene_lock.color_palette: Blue, white, black, silver metal, gray ground, neutral daylight tones.
consistency_phrases: Preserve the exact bicycle identity, colors, and geometry across all shots
Keep the front V-brake and front suspension fork visually consistent
Show only the front wheel as damaged and keep the rear wheel normally inflated
Keep the outdoor background and daylight consistent from start to finish
negative_cues: complete bicycle visible in the background while working on the removed wheel
two bicycle wheels in the same shot during tire repair
intact bike frame behind the loose wheel

steps.title: Release the front brake and remove the front wheel
steps.description: Squeeze the front V-brake arms together, release the brake noodle, open the quick release, and remove the front wheel from the fork.
steps.subject_focus: The front V-brake, brake noodle, quick-release lever, and fork dropouts.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: Outdoor repair setting with the same blue-and-white Decathlon Rockrider bike in front of a white corrugated wall. Subject: Black nitrile-gloved hands squeeze the front V-brake arms and release the brake noodle. Details: Photorealistic, accurate V-brake geometry, natural daylight, crisp mechanical detail. Constraints: Preserve the exact bike identity and show the front wheel as the damaged wheel.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: Same outdoor setting. Subject: The front wheel is lowered out of the blue suspension fork after the quick release has been opened. Details: Photorealistic close-up, realistic axle alignment, rear wheel remains mounted and normally inflated. Constraints: No disc brake, no extra broken parts.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the hands squeezing the V-brake arms, lifting out the brake noodle, opening the quick-release lever, and guiding the front wheel down out of the fork with natural mechanical motion.
steps.narration: First, release the front V-brake, open the quick release, and remove the front wheel from the fork.
steps.subtitle: Release brake, remove wheel.
steps.safety_note: Make sure the bicycle is stable before removing the wheel.
steps.success_criteria: The front wheel is fully removed and the brake is released.
steps.common_mistake: Trying to remove the wheel without releasing the V-brake first.

steps.title: Unseat one side of the tire
steps.description: Make sure the tire is fully deflated, then use two plastic tire levers to lift one tire bead over the rim and free one side of the tire.
steps.subject_focus: The tire bead, silver rim, and tire levers.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: TIGHT macro framing of ONLY the removed front wheel laid flat on rough gray concrete. The bicycle frame and the rear wheel are NOT visible in this shot — the wheel is isolated, the bike is parked out of frame. Background is plain gray concrete and the bottom edge of the white corrugated metal wall. Subject: Gloved hands press the flat front tire to remove any remaining air and insert a blue plastic tire lever under the black tire bead on the silver rim. Details: Photorealistic macro detail, realistic rubber texture, visible flattened tire profile. Constraints: Use plastic tire levers only; bike body absent from the frame.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: SAME tight macro framing — only the isolated front wheel on the gray concrete, bike NOT in frame. Subject: One side of the black tire bead is lifted outside the silver rim while a second tire lever helps free a larger section. Details: Macro mechanical detail, believable rubber tension, accurate wheel proportions. Constraints: No screwdriver, no unrealistic deformation, no bicycle body in the background.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the lever sliding under the tire bead, prying it up, and the bead gradually popping over the rim in a controlled, realistic motion.
steps.narration: Fully deflate the tire, then use plastic tire levers to lift one side of the tire off the rim.
steps.subtitle: Lever one bead over the rim.
steps.safety_note: Never use a screwdriver because it can damage the tube or the rim.
steps.success_criteria: One full side of the tire is free from the rim.
steps.common_mistake: Pinching the inner tube while levering the tire.

steps.title: Remove the punctured inner tube and inspect the tire
steps.description: Pull the old inner tube out of the tire, then inspect the inside of the tire and the tread to find and remove the thorn, glass, or sharp debris that caused the puncture.
steps.subject_focus: The old inner tube and the inside of the tire.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: SAME tight macro framing — only the isolated front wheel on the gray concrete; the rest of the bicycle is OUT of frame. Subject: Gloved hands pull a deflated black inner tube out of the opened front tire and remove the valve from the rim hole. Details: Photorealistic documentary style, realistic floppy rubber, accurate wheel detail. Constraints: No bicycle frame or rear wheel anywhere in the shot.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: SAME tight macro framing — only the isolated front wheel on gray concrete; bike NOT in frame. Subject: The old inner tube has been REMOVED and is OFF-FRAME entirely. Gloved fingertips trace the INSIDE of the open tire casing, feeling along the inner rubber for thorns or sharp debris. Details: Photorealistic macro view, fingertips clearly INSIDE the tire interior, clear tire texture. Constraints: NO inner tube visible anywhere (it has been removed and discarded off-frame); the focus is the internal tire INSPECTION action.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the old tube being removed smoothly, then transition to a careful tire inspection with gloved fingers tracing the inside of the casing and checking the tread area.
steps.narration: Remove the punctured tube, then carefully inspect the inside of the tire so the new tube does not get punctured again.
steps.subtitle: Remove tube, inspect tire.
steps.safety_note: Be careful because sharp debris may still be inside the tire.
steps.success_criteria: The old tube is removed and the tire interior is free of sharp debris.
steps.common_mistake: Skipping the inspection and getting another puncture immediately.

steps.title: Install the new inner tube
steps.description: Lightly inflate the replacement tube so it keeps its shape, insert the valve through the rim hole, and place the tube evenly inside the tire without twists or folds.
steps.subject_focus: The valve insertion and the even placement of the new tube.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: SAME tight macro framing — only the isolated front wheel on the gray concrete; bicycle body OUT of frame. Subject: A new black inner tube with slight pre-inflation is guided by gloved hands, and its valve is inserted through the rim hole of the front wheel. Details: Photorealistic close-up, precise alignment, silver rim and black tire clearly visible. Constraints: The valve must sit straight; no bicycle frame in the background.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: SAME tight macro framing — only the isolated front wheel; bike NOT in frame. Subject: Gloved hands tuck the slightly inflated inner tube evenly inside the open tire all around the rim. Details: Photorealistic, natural daylight, no twists, no bulges, correct tire geometry. Constraints: The tube must be fully inside the tire and not trapped under the bead; no bicycle visible.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the valve entering the rim hole cleanly, followed by careful hand movements placing the tube uniformly inside the tire.
steps.narration: Lightly inflate the new tube, insert the valve, and place the tube evenly inside the tire.
steps.subtitle: Insert new tube evenly.
steps.safety_note: Check that the tube is straight and not twisted before closing the tire.
steps.success_criteria: The valve is straight and the tube is evenly seated inside the tire.
steps.common_mistake: Installing the tube with folds or twists.

steps.title: Close the tire and inflate it
steps.description: Push the tire bead back into the rim by hand, check that the tube is not pinched, then use the pump to inflate the tire to the recommended pressure written on the sidewall.
steps.subject_focus: The final bead seating and the inflation process.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: SAME tight macro framing — only the isolated front wheel on gray concrete; bicycle body OUT of frame. Subject: Gloved THUMBS press the LAST section of the black tire bead back over the silver rim by HAND, working it section by section. NO PUMP in the frame yet — bare hands only. Details: Photorealistic close-up, visible hand strain, bead-seating action. Constraints: NO bicycle pump anywhere in this shot; ONLY hands manually seating the last bead.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: SAME tight macro framing — only the isolated front wheel on gray concrete; bike NOT in frame. Subject: A floor pump's hose is CONNECTED to the valve and the tire is being inflated, currently HALF-INFLATED (clearly mid-action, not flat and not fully round). Details: Photorealistic, pump body visible, pump hose attached to valve, tire profile shows partial inflation in progress. Constraints: Show the pump ACTIVELY inflating; the tire must visibly be partially round — not flat, not fully inflated.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the last section of the tire bead being pushed into place, followed by rhythmic pump strokes and the front tire gradually expanding from flat to fully inflated.
steps.narration: Close the tire by hand, make sure the tube is not pinched, and inflate the tire to the recommended pressure.
steps.subtitle: Seat bead, inflate tire.
steps.safety_note: Watch the tire while inflating to make sure the bead seats evenly all the way around.
steps.success_criteria: The tire is fully seated, centered, and inflated to a normal riding shape.
steps.common_mistake: Inflating the tire while part of the tube is trapped under the bead.

steps.title: Reinstall the wheel and reconnect the brake
steps.description: Place the front wheel back into the fork, secure the quick release, reconnect the V-brake noodle, and confirm that the wheel spins freely and the brake works correctly.
steps.subject_focus: The axle seating, quick release, and front V-brake reconnection.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: Same outdoor setting. SINGLE PHOTOGRAPHIC MOMENT — NOT a multi-panel illustration, NOT a tutorial collage. Subject: The repaired front wheel is being guided back into the empty blue suspension fork by gloved hands, aligning the axle with the fork dropouts. Details: Photorealistic single frame, properly inflated tire, accurate fork geometry, natural shadows. Constraints: ONE photograph of a single moment in time; ABSOLUTELY NO sub-panels, NO grid layout, NO before-and-after split.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: Same outdoor setting. SINGLE PHOTOGRAPHIC MOMENT — NOT a montage. Subject: Gloved hands reconnect the front V-brake noodle on the fully reassembled bike. Both wheels are mounted and properly inflated. Details: Photorealistic single frame, front V-brake re-attached, hands clearly on the brake assembly. Constraints: ONE photograph; NO multi-panel layout, NO before-and-after grid.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the wheel sliding into the fork, the quick-release lever closing firmly, the brake noodle being reconnected, and finish with a subtle final confirmation shot of the repaired bicycle.
steps.narration: Put the front wheel back into the fork, secure it, reconnect the brake, and check that everything works before riding.
steps.subtitle: Refit wheel, reconnect brake.
steps.safety_note: Always test the front brake before riding the bicycle.
steps.success_criteria: The wheel is secure, the tire is inflated, the wheel spins straight, and the brake functions normally.
steps.common_mistake: Forgetting to reconnect the front brake before riding away.
